_notes: Each entry is one destination/stop. recommended_nights is a default suggestion, not fixed. sub_itinerary is optional - use it when a stop is really a base for a multi-day excursion (e.g. Cusco -> Salkantay Trek) so the trek can be shown distinctly from town time. vibe_scores are 0-10, used for matching searches like 'ski trip' or 'romantic'. wandrly_notes are short first-person tips - shown in a distinct UI box. tom_verdict is one or two punchy sentences in your voice.

destinations.name: Colombo
destinations.summary: Chaotic gateway city with great street food

activities.name: Galle Face Green
activities.desc: Sunset walk along the seafront esplanade, street food stalls come alive in the evening

activities.name: Pettah Market
activities.desc: Sensory overload market district — explore the spice and textile lanes

activities.name: Dutch Hospital precinct
activities.desc: Restored colonial quarter with good cafes if you need a calm reset

food_recommendations: Street food stalls along Galle Face Green at sunset
Pettah market for spices and snacks
nightlife: Quiet compared to the rest of the trip — mostly hotel bars
transport_tips: Fly into Bandaranaike Airport (CMB), about 40 minutes by tuk-tuk to the centre
warnings: Traffic can make short trips take longer than expected
destinations.tom_verdict: Not a highlight, but a fine one-night landing pad before heading inland.

destinations.name: Sigiriya
destinations.summary: Ancient rock fortress and cave temples

activities.name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
activities.desc: Climb at sunrise before the tour groups arrive — frescoes and mirror wall are highlights

activities.name: Pidurangala Rock
activities.desc: Hike up for the best view of Sigiriya itself, with a fraction of the crowd

activities.name: Dambulla Cave Temple
activities.desc: 150 Buddha statues across 5 caves — the largest cave temple complex on the island

food_recommendations: Two-storey tourist restaurants in town are reliable and good value
nightlife: Very quiet — early nights here are normal
transport_tips: Train from Colombo to Habarana then a short tuk-tuk, or a direct bus (about 4 hours)
warnings: Elephants do roam near farmland at night — listen for alarms/shooting sounds, this is normal elephant deterrence
wandrly_notes: Sigiriya is basically a must-do — climb in the morning pre-heat and pre-crowds, arrive by 7am at the latest.
Pidurangala is a scramble to get up but gorgeous views and gives the best photo of Sigiriya itself.
Stop in Dambulla on the way for the temples and the central fruit market — both surprisingly cool.
destinations.tom_verdict: One of the best mornings of the whole trip. Don't skip Pidurangala just because Sigiriya is the famous one.

destinations.name: Kandy
destinations.summary: Sacred hill city with cooler air

activities.name: Temple of the Tooth
activities.desc: Visit during the early morning puja ceremony for the most authentic experience

activities.name: Kandy Lake loop
activities.desc: Easy 45-minute walk around the lake and through the old town

activities.name: Peradeniya Botanical Gardens
activities.desc: 60 acres of colonial-era gardens including a striking bamboo cathedral

nightlife: Limited — this is a calmer, more spiritual stop
transport_tips: Scenic train from Habarana/Sigiriya area — book first class in advance if possible
similar_to: Luang Prabang for the spiritual hill-town feel
destinations.tom_verdict: A good change of pace after Sigiriya — cooler, calmer, and a nice cultural anchor.

destinations.name: Ella
destinations.summary: Tea hills, hikes and the famous train line

activities.name: Nine Arches Bridge
activities.desc: Arrive around 8:15am to catch the train crossing for the classic photo

activities.name: Little Adam's Peak
activities.desc: Easy one-hour hike with sweeping views over the tea plantations

activities.name: Ella Rock
activities.desc: A tougher three-hour hike with far fewer tourists than Adam's Peak

food_recommendations: Cafes along the main strip do good Western/Sri Lankan fusion
nightlife: Small but social backpacker bar scene
transport_tips: The Kandy to Ella train is one of the great rail journeys — book well ahead, views are stunning from Nanu-Oya onward
similar_to: Pai (Thailand) for the laid-back hill-town backpacker vibe
wandrly_notes: Walk to Rawana Cave behind the falls — almost nobody goes there, it's a great quiet add-on.
destinations.tom_verdict: Easily worth 3 nights — the hiking, the train, and the chill cafe scene all genuinely deliver.

destinations.name: Mirissa
destinations.summary: South coast beach town, whales and sunsets

activities.name: Whale watching
activities.desc: Boats leave early morning, blue and sperm whales possible December to April

activities.name: Secret Beach
activities.desc: A 20 minute walk east along the coast from the main beach — mostly locals here

activities.name: Beachside seafood
activities.desc: Grilled catch of the day at the beachfront restaurants, best at sunset

food_recommendations: Beachfront seafood grills, best at sunset
transport_tips: Bus from Ella, or tuk-tuk from Matara train station, roughly 3 hours
warnings: Whale watching only worthwhile Dec-April — check season before planning around it
wandrly_notes: Secret Beach is a 20 minute walk east of the main beach — mostly locals, much nicer than the main strip.
destinations.tom_verdict: Good for slowing down after the hill country — the whale watching is genuinely worth the early start in season.

destinations.name: Galle
destinations.summary: UNESCO Dutch fort town, the most atmospheric stop

activities.name: Galle Fort ramparts
activities.desc: Walk the sea walls at sunset for the best light and ocean views

activities.name: Fort cafes and galleries
activities.desc: Small lanes inside the fort have excellent independent cafes and shops

activities.name: Jungle Beach
activities.desc: A short tuk-tuk ride north to a hidden cove, good for snorkelling

food_recommendations: Coconut sambal and the 'street burger' (a KFC-style local favourite)
nightlife: Quiet, mostly cafe/bar culture inside the fort
transport_tips: Coastal train from Mirissa/Matara — the clifftop section is one of the best short rides on the island
warnings: Fort walk is hot in the middle of the day — go morning or sunset
similar_to: Cartagena (Colombia) for the colonial fort-town feel
wandrly_notes: Stayed just outside the fort for cheaper, larger accommodation — only a $5 tuk-tuk into the fort itself.
Diving at Hikkaduwa (20 mins away) is bare-bones but cheap — around 80 USD for 2 dives plus gear.
destinations.tom_verdict: The most atmospheric stop on the island. Worth the full walk around the fort walls even though it's hot.

destinations.name: Tbilisi
destinations.summary: Quirky capital with old town charm and great food

activities.name: Old Town wander
activities.desc: Narrow streets, balconies and sulfur bathhouses in the historic district

activities.name: Narikala Fortress
activities.desc: Take the cable car up for sunset views over the whole city

activities.name: Dezerter Bazaar
activities.desc: Huge local market — great for trying Georgian cheeses, bread and spices

food_recommendations: Khachapuri (cheese bread) everywhere — try a few regional styles
nightlife: Genuinely excellent and very cheap — a real highlight
transport_tips: Tbilisi International Airport is about 20 minutes from the centre
destinations.tom_verdict: One of the best value capitals in the world right now — go before everyone else figures it out.

destinations.name: Kazbegi (Stepantsminda)
destinations.summary: Dramatic mountain village beneath Mount Kazbek

activities.name: Gergeti Trinity Church
activities.desc: A scenic 2-3 hour hike up to a church with Mount Kazbek as the backdrop

activities.name: Truso Valley walk
activities.desc: A quieter alternative hike through an abandoned valley near the Russian border

activities.name: Local guesthouse dinner
activities.desc: Home-cooked Georgian feasts are common and excellent value here

food_recommendations: Home-cooked guesthouse dinners — often included in the room price
transport_tips: Shared marshrutka (minibus) from Tbilisi takes about 3 hours along the dramatic Georgian Military Highway
warnings: Weather can close the pass — build in flexibility
similar_to: Huaraz (Peru) for the dramatic peak-town setting
destinations.tom_verdict: The single best mountain backdrop of the whole Georgia loop — worth the bumpy drive.

destinations.name: Sighnaghi
destinations.summary: Wine country town with sweeping valley views

activities.name: Wine cellar tours
activities.desc: Kakheti is Georgia's wine heartland — small family cellars offer tastings of qvevri wine

activities.name: City walls walk
activities.desc: Sighnaghi's walls offer views over the Alazani Valley toward the Caucasus

activities.name: Bodbe Monastery
activities.desc: A short walk from town, peaceful grounds and valley views

food_recommendations: Family-run wine cellars often pair tastings with home-cooked food
nightlife: Very quiet — this is a wind-down stop
transport_tips: Marshrutka or shared taxi from Tbilisi, around 2.5 hours through the wine region
similar_to: Stellenbosch (South Africa) for the small wine-town feel
destinations.tom_verdict: A great slow finish to the loop — the valley views from the walls are some of the best in the country.

destinations.name: Christchurch

activities.name: Botanic Gardens
activities.desc: A relaxed first stop to stretch your legs after the flight

activities.name: Street art tour
activities.desc: Central Christchurch has excellent post-earthquake street art scattered through the CBD

food_recommendations: Riverside Market in the CBD for a good food hall fix
nightlife: Decent for a smaller city — concentrated in the CBD
transport_tips: Main South Island international airport — pick up a rental car here
destinations.tom_verdict: Not a destination in itself, but a perfectly good 1-2 night bookend for a South Island loop.

destinations.name: Lake Tekapo
destinations.summary: Turquoise lake beneath the Southern Alps

activities.name: Church of the Good Shepherd
activities.desc: Iconic small stone church right on the lakeshore, best at sunrise

activities.name: Stargazing
activities.desc: Part of a Dark Sky Reserve — book a stargazing tour if the weather is clear

food_recommendations: Limited options — the lakeside cafe near the church is the main one
transport_tips: About 3 hours drive from Christchurch through the Canterbury Plains
warnings: A short stop is enough — most people use this as a one-night scenic break
destinations.tom_verdict: One night is right — see the church, do the stargazing if clear, then move on.

destinations.name: Wanaka
destinations.summary: Lakeside town, less crowded than Queenstown

activities.name: Isthmus Peak hike
activities.desc: A quieter alternative to Roys Peak with equally stunning lake views

activities.name: Rob Roy Glacier track
activities.desc: A relatively easy walk to a hanging glacier and waterfalls

activities.name: That Wanaka Tree
activities.desc: The famous lone tree in the lake — best at sunrise to avoid crowds

food_recommendations: Lakefront cafes around the Wanaka Tree area
nightlife: A handful of good bars, calmer than Queenstown
transport_tips: About 3 hours from Lake Tekapo via Lindis Pass — one of the most scenic drives in the country
similar_to: Queenstown but quieter — a good alternative base
wandrly_notes: Skip the busy Roys Peak track and try Isthmus Peak instead — similar views, far fewer people.
destinations.tom_verdict: My pick over Queenstown for a base — same scenery, fraction of the crowds.

destinations.name: Queenstown

activities.name: Ben Lomond via Tiki Trail
activities.desc: Free hiking route up the mountain with better views than the paid gondola

activities.name: Milford Sound day trip
activities.desc: A long day but the fiord scenery is genuinely unmatched — go early to beat tour buses

activities.name: Queenstown Gardens
activities.desc: A free, relaxed walk right by the lake if you need a lower-key day

food_recommendations: Fergburger is touristy but genuinely good — go early or late to skip the queue
nightlife: The best in the South Island — busy every night of the week in season
transport_tips: About 1 hour from Wanaka via the Crown Range, the highest sealed road in NZ
warnings: Prices spike heavily in peak season — book accommodation well ahead
wandrly_notes: Skip the paid gondola — the free Tiki Trail up Ben Lomond has better views for $0.
destinations.tom_verdict: Worth the hype for the activities, but base in Wanaka if budget or crowds are a concern.

destinations.name: Cusco
destinations.summary: Gateway to the Sacred Valley — most visitors use this as a base for the Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu

activities.name: San Pedro Market
activities.desc: Huge local market — great for breakfast and people-watching before acclimatising

activities.name: Plaza de Armas & Cathedral
activities.desc: The historic centre, best visited in your first day or two while adjusting to altitude

activities.name: Sacsayhuamán
activities.desc: Massive Inca stonework ruins a short walk above the city, good easy-altitude activity

food_recommendations: San Pedro Market for cheap, authentic breakfast
nightlife: A lively backpacker bar scene around the Plaza de Armas
transport_tips: Most international connections route through Lima first — domestic flight or bus to Cusco
warnings: Altitude sickness is common — spend 1-2 days acclimatising in Cusco before starting any trek
similar_to: Kathmandu as a trek gateway city

sub_itinerary.name: Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu
sub_itinerary.summary: A 5-day trek from Cusco's Sacred Valley over the Salkantay Pass (4,650m) to Machu Picchu — the most popular alternative to the Inca Trail

days.title: Cusco to Soraypampa
days.desc: Drive from Cusco, then trek to the base camp beneath Salkantay mountain

days.title: Salkantay Pass to Chaullay
days.desc: The toughest day — cross the 4,650m pass, then descend into cloud forest

days.title: Chaullay to La Playa / Santa Teresa
days.desc: Continue descending through coffee and fruit plantations

days.title: Hidroeléctrica to Aguas Calientes
days.desc: Walk the train tracks to the town beneath Machu Picchu

days.title: Machu Picchu
days.desc: Early entry to Machu Picchu itself, then train/bus back to Cusco

destinations.tom_verdict: If you're booking 'Cusco', know that most of those nights will actually be spent on the trek — plan the city time around acclimatisation, not sightseeing.
